reviews.review_msg: Hardest elective I have taken, nice teacher but the tests/assignments are strictly graded.

She gave many chances to get bonus credit and her lectures were interesting. However, there is a substantial amount of readings to complete weekly. I suggest showing up to every class and actually participating because she WILL give you bad grades for not doing so and she is a very hard marker. THIS IS NOT A GPA BOOSTER

Absolutely wonderful class and Dr. Higgins is a great lecturer. She is very informed on her discipline, and very engaging. Watch out for the things she emphasizes in lecture, because they will be on the midterm. Her option to submit papers early for feedback was a life saver. Really rewarding and great course if you're willing to put in the work!

Dr. Higgins is one of the best profs I have ever had at sfu. She cares about her students and their wellbeing and ensures that her lectures are entertaining. She gives great feedback on all the writing and tests are reflective of the lecture material. She is very approachable and makes you want to go to all of her classes because it's interesting.

I took a Directed Reading with her and three other students about Coptic. The language is incredibly difficult (not helped by a confusing textbook), but Dr. Higgins was able to help us learn as much as possible in this course. I would highly recommend her for any other class, and I hope to take more from her in the future.

Seriously amazing prof -- the only class where I actually wanted to attend lectures. You can really tell she cares about the material and she truly wants her students to do well (and gives opportunities to boost your grade!). Exams are easy if you engage with lectures. Taking two additional courses with her this semester because she was so lovely.

Extremely passionate prof who offers many opportunities for students to do well and improve their mark. Textbook isn't necessary so long as you attend the lectures, which are very engaging, in both the prof's attitude towards teaching and the content itself. Fun variety of assignments as well, but a bit of a tough marker.

Professor Higgins is awesome! She relates her personal experiences in the field of archeology to the topic whenever appropriate. She doesn't rely on only papers for project grades. We did multimedia projects for my class. She has "required" reading but not really since the readings are to contextualize the topic of study.
Great prof, but I did not expect the emphasis on participation. That said, I will retake her class :)
Great lectures as she cares about the material. She marks her assignments fairly as well as the midterm for this course. The only negative in this course is a decent amount of library research.

Nice prof but she grades papers on an unreachable scale. Her lectures are very informative and engaging, but it's content heavy. If you have no basic understanding of ancient history, this is NOT the course for you!

Such a fun professor, gives amazing lectures and the textbook lines up perfectly with her lecture schedule. Definitely would suggest going to class because she goes over practice questions for exams. She gives really good feedback on papers and lets you submit them before they're due for comments which was so helpful

Best prof at SFU and a really interesting course. She'll work so hard to help you if you're willing to put the work in. You will have to study a lot (know specific details and examples for exams) and the final paper is so long, but if you work hard and ask for help you'll get an A no problem. Such a nice person and her lectures are really fun.
Dont take her.
A delightful teacher - a stickler for proper citations and sources, gives hard work but is an excellent teacher and loves her material.

She's a really great proff and an engaging lecturer. Her passion for the subject matter really shines through and you can tell she genuinely cares about her students. She is very accessible and willing to help! you definitely need to attend lectures, but honestly, I looked forward to her lectures!

You won't find a prof who cares more about her students or works harder than Sabrina. The content isn't terribly difficult, but there is a lot of work involved. Plenty of opportunities for extra credit and she will look over your paper for detailed feedback if you submit it 2 weeks early. She really does everything she can to help you succeed.

get ready for a 4000 words essay. Really nice prof. If you put in the time to work and ask her questions, she is accommodating and accessible. Only take this class if you are good at English. Extremely strict on grammar. One of the best lecturer I have had, really engaging and fun. Exams are fair. Take this class if you want to have fun.

I haven't yet finished the semester with Sabrina, however she is single handedly one of the greatest profs I've had at SFU. She's so insanely engaging, and so passionate about the topics she teaches; you can't help but pay attention. One of the kindest people I've ever met, she wants students to succeed (start your term paper as early as you can!)

Great lectures and very into the material and such. You definitely would need to attend for notes (she also lets you know what will be on the midterm and final, like hey know this). gives lots of commentary on papers and midterms. great prof all round

Sabrina is one of the best profs I have taken in my 5 years at Simon Fraser University. She gets students engaged on the topic through her inspirational passion towards the subject. This was the second course I've taken with Sabrina, and 100% would take another one if given the chance.
Best prof I ever had! Also cuz the course was easy, but actually, great person, great prof.

Solid professor. Reading material well-related to the lectures. Exams were fair and she was lenient with marking. If you knew at least some things about the architecture that was shown, you could get part-marks. Take good notes on the readings, go to lecture, study, and you will succeed. She's really nice as well.

An amazing professor. She's very happy, very knowledgeable and passionate of her research, and she gives a lot of extra credit. Her final exams and midterms are very easy, and she has a clear grading criteria for all the assignments. I would take her again in a heartbeat.

So happy even on Monday mornings! Sooo much to cover, but she gives u hints about what to remember for exams. Extra credit possible if u go to a night lecture (she tells the class about them before) and write it up. Lots of slides to memorize about what they are and what's important about them.
